title: Divorce / Legal Separation
summary: Move from first decision to filing readiness: case type, venue, disclosure, parenting, support, property, service, and decree terms.
phases: Confirm divorce, legal separation, or annulment path
Collect income, debt, asset, and property records
Map parenting, child support, and spousal maintenance issues
Prepare filing, service, response, and decree-readiness questions
listener: Any court date or response deadline?
Any safety issue or protective order?
Any home, retirement, or debt issue?

title: Parenting Time / Legal Decision-Making
summary: Build a practical parenting record: current schedule, decision-making concerns, child needs, school records, exchange problems, and proposed plan.
formsLabel: Official child support and family-law forms
phases: Document the current schedule and missed-time issues
List legal decision-making concerns and child-specific needs
Gather school, daycare, medical, and communication records
Draft proposed schedule, exchange, holiday, and dispute terms
listener: Is another state or tribal court involved?

title: Child Support
summary: Organize the inputs that usually drive support review: income, insurance, childcare, parenting days, existing orders, arrears, and DCSS involvement.
formsLabel: Arizona child support and family-law forms
phases: Identify whether support is new, modified, enforced, or reviewed
Collect income, insurance, childcare, and parenting-time inputs
Review existing orders, arrears, payment records, and DCSS status
Prepare questions for worksheet, filing, or enforcement review

title: Modification / Enforcement
summary: Turn an existing order problem into a reviewable packet: signed orders, changed facts, missed obligations, evidence, deadlines, and requested result.
phases: Locate every signed order and minute entry
Separate modification facts from enforcement facts
Collect proof: payment records, messages, calendars, police or school records
Identify the specific order or result you want reviewed
listener: Is this change, enforcement, or both?

title: Document Prep / Filing
summary: Get from scattered paperwork to a reviewable filing packet: document type, court status, copies, exhibits, service, and filing readiness.
phases: Identify the filing type and whether a case already exists
Gather required forms, exhibits, notices, and proposed orders
Check signatures, copies, service needs, and court deadline issues
Route to document preparation, review, or filing support
listener: Do you need review or preparation?

title: Not Sure Where to Start
summary: Use this triage path when the issue, form, deadline, or service type is unclear. The goal is to sort the starting point before choosing forms.
phases: Identify whether a case exists or needs to be started
List any deadline, court date, served papers, or safety issue
Sort the issue into divorce, parenting, support, orders, documents, or scope review
Use Guided Intake to preserve facts for review and next-step routing
listener: Do you have papers from court?
Are you unsure which form applies?
